data.title: Group 1 Automotive Q4 earnings missed estimates by -9.0%, EPS $8.49 vs. estimate $9.37. Revenue up $34M from previous year.
data.description: The Q4 earnings report for Group 1 Automotive (NYSE: GPI) was released on Thursday, January 29, 2026, at 05:01 AM. The company fell short of estimated earnings by -9.0%, with an EPS of $8.49 compared to the expected $9.37. Revenue showed an increase of $34.00 million from the previous year. This continues a trend of the company missing EPS estimates in previous quarters. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Southside Bancshares Q4 earnings missed estimates by -11%, EPS at $0.7 vs $0.79. Revenue down $3.16M from last year.
data.description: Southside Bancshares (NYSE: SBSI) released their Q4 earnings report on Thursday, January 29, 2026, at 05:45 AM. The company fell short of estimated earnings by -11.0%, posting an EPS of $0.7 compared to the expected $0.79. Revenue decreased by $3.16 million from the previous year. For additional details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Sei Network issues 2026 Validator Guide as Solana's validators decrease by 65%. Key info for stakers on choosing trustworthy validators.
data.description: Sei has released detailed guidelines for selecting validators, coinciding with a 65% decline in Solana's validator count. This underscores the importance for stakers to understand how to choose trustworthy validators. For more information, click on the link provided.

data.title: Metaplanet secures $137M for Bitcoin purchase and debt repayment in capital strategy move.
data.description: Metaplanet announced that the funds raised will go towards purchasing Bitcoin, investing in its Bitcoin income business, and making partial repayments on its debts as part of its capital strategy.

data.title: GOMSO launches 'Kkini-Kkini' K-Umami Sticks, 1,000-year JOTGAL fermented seafood for global foodies.
data.description: South Korea's renowned fermentation hub, GOMSO, famous for its 1,000-year JOTGAL brand, has introduced 'Kkini-Kkini' for global food enthusiasts. The new product is a portable fermented seafood aimed at appealing to a worldwide audience. This announcement was made on January 29, 2026, in Seoul.

data.title: USD1 from World Liberty Financial reaches over $5 billion market cap as TRUMP meme coin falters amid decreasing interest.
data.description: The USD1 stablecoin, associated with former U.S. President Trump, is experiencing a significant increase in value due to institutional adoption. Meanwhile, the official meme coin linked to the former President is facing a decrease in popularity as hype surrounding it declines.

data.description: The Sei Network has outlined its rewards framework within its ecosystem as the cryptocurrency SEI is currently trading at $0.1055. The network's credibility has received a significant boost by its recent integration with Orbs and partnership with a validator from Bhutan.

data.title: Trump admin abandons minimum price support for US critical minerals projects due to funding issues. No response from White House yet. MP Materials price floor set last July remains unchanged. Policy shift affects future deals.
data.description: The Trump administration has reportedly decided to backtrack on plans to ensure a minimum price for U.S. critical minerals projects due to potential lack of congressional funding and market pricing complexities. In a closed-door meeting with U.S. minerals executives in Washington, officials emphasized that projects should be financially sustainable without government price support. This policy change will impact future agreements but does not affect the established price floor for MP Materials (NYSE: MP) set in July. This decision contradicts previous assurances from officials, indicating the price floor for MP Materials was not a temporary measure. The White House has yet to respond to Benzinga's request for comment. For more details, please refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
